_README: DrawSteelGlyphs/Wingdings codepoint -> the text it resolves to, used BOTH when building the fidelity wordbag (resolve.py + build_wordbag.py) AND as the convention the markdown converter renders. Empty string = decorative (dropped, contributes no word). Keys starting with '_' are documentation and ignored by resolve.py. Verified visually against 'The Summoner v1.0b' PDF on 2026-06-09. The fidelity gate validates this map end-to-end: a mis-mapped glyph makes the slice/chunk fidelity check fail, so errors surface rather than hide. See GLYPHS.md for the annotated table and revisit notes.

_notes.characteristics: 0x4d/0x6d=M(ight), 0x41/0x61=A(gility), 0x52/0x72=R(eason), 0x49/0x69=I(ntuition), 0x50/0x70=P(resence). The glyph is the capital initial; in stat blocks the rest of the word follows in normal text (so the glyph JOINS the next characters -> 'Might'); in formulas it stands alone ('R damage', '2 + R'). Both upper- and lower-case codepoints render as the uppercase letter (potency badges show 'I<WEAK').
_notes.potency: Rendered '<CHAR> < <STRENGTH-or-VALUE>' to match Draw Steel house style (cf. heroes/beastheart 'M < AVERAGE'). 0x3c='<'. Strength words: 0x77=weak, 0x76=average, 0x73=strong. Numeric potency uses the digit glyphs 0x31-0x35 (the book prints numbers for some abilities and strength-words for others; render whichever the page shows -- the values are intentionally different).
_notes.decorative: 0xa2 (x215, ability/keyword chevron), 0xa3, 0xa5 (charge mark before 'N charge:'), 0xae (small-caps section ornament e.g. before 'Summoner Advancement'), 0x5d (badge end-cap), w:0x8a (Wingdings star bullet) are dropped as non-words. NOTE (per user 2026-06-09): some of these MAY be statblock ability-type icons that carry information rather than pure decoration; they are dropped for now because they are not prose words and dropping them does not affect word-fidelity. Revisit here if a statblock needs one of these to convey meaning.
